title: Bring anything — neutralizes the vibe-filter for hesitant visitors
problem: The VibeFilter section ('je fais / je fais pas') answers the 'is this for me?' question with explicit do/don't lists. A side effect: a hesitant visitor who sees an item they're unsure about on the 'don't' side may self-filter out incorrectly. BringAnything sits IMMEDIATELY after VibeFilter to neutralize that self-filter — Marc's framing is 'I want every idea to come in; the triage is my job, not the visitor's.' The examples are aspirational, not historical, since the practice hasn't built a long history yet.
analysis: DICT[lang].bringAnything content block. Sits right after VibeFilter in Home.tsx render order, by design — its job is to undo the over-filter the vibe section can cause. Examples are aspirational ('things I'd happily take') not 'projects I have shipped'. Feature-coloured as 'intake' (HOME_SECTION_FEATURE['bring-anything']) since it pushes the visitor toward the intake form.

decisions.decision: Sits directly after VibeFilter, by render order
decisions.rationale: BringAnything's purpose is to neutralize VibeFilter's self-filter effect. The two sections must be adjacent and in order; reordering them breaks the funnel logic.
decisions.recommendation: Don't reorder these two. If a future audit suggests a different funnel structure, evaluate whether BringAnything still has its purpose; if so, keep adjacent.

decisions.rationale: The practice hasn't built a multi-year client history yet. Falsely-historic examples would be dishonest; aspirational examples ('things I'd happily take') are structurally true.
decisions.recommendation: Once the showcase has 6+ shipped projects, revisit — maybe the section can use real anonymized examples. Until then, aspirational copy.

successCriteria:
- Renders directly after VibeFilter in home order
- Copy positions Marc as the triager, not the visitor
- Examples are aspirational (not falsely historic)
- Bilingual at full parity
- Feature-coloured as intake (pushes toward intake form)
implementation: src/components/BringAnything.tsx — DICT[lang].bringAnything. SectionEyebrow + HOME_FOLIOS for chrome.
userGuide: Edit copy via DICT.fr.bringAnything / DICT.en.bringAnything. If reordering Home sections, keep BringAnything immediately after VibeFilter.
knownLimitations: Examples may go stale; revisit periodically
No CTA button (intentional — the section is messaging, not conversion)

statusHistory.reason: Component shipped to neutralize VibeFilter's over-filter.
Code-first doc co-located. Adjacency-to-VibeFilter decision stable.
